title: Palicourea cymosa
taxonomy: Palicourea cymosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Palicourea and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This species is closely related to other members of the Rubiaceae family, which includes plants such as gardenias and coffee plants. Further classification of Palicourea cymosa may include its placement within the tribe Palicoureeae. The specific classification of this plant can be found in various taxonomic databases and botanical resources.

It has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. intro: Palicourea cymosa, also known as "palo amarillo", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea cymosa include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Palicourea cymosa is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various health conditions, and its leaves are also used in traditional remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
